text:
Revolio Clockberg, Jr., more commonly referred to as Gearhead, is a recurring character who first appeared in the episode "Ricksy Business". His character is a representative of "social gearheads", people who know a lot of technical information, in Gearhead's case, it's information about the Gear Wars and engineering. Despite his and Rick's shaky relationship, they're still able to maintain a friendship, with Gearhead being invited to multiple parties hosted by Rick.

Gearhead is first seen as a guest at Rick and Summer's party, explaining the Gear Wars to Rick, as Morty anxiously paces the house. To escape talking to Gearhead, Rick calls Morty over and uses him as a distraction. Gearhead then begins to tell Morty about the Gear Wars. Later in the episode, he is seen playing a musical instrument similar to a lute to an admiring crowd, one of the onlookers being Jessica.

He makes his second appearance in "Mortynight Run", where he helps fix Rick's Shipsaucer. But when he finds out that the "Fart" in Rick's custody is priceless, Gearhead betrays Rick, pulling a gun on him. He reveals his resentment toward Rick, and that the nickname "Gearhead" is tantamount to a racial slur. Rick kicks him in his "gearsticles", then removes them and switches them with the gears in his mouth.

Gearhead appears again at the end of "Vindicators 3: The Return of Worldender", as a guest at Rick's party for Noob-Noob. Rick apparently ended his feud with Gearhead after getting high with him. In the post-credits scene for the episode, Gearhead uses Morty's Vindicators jacket to pick up girls. When an actual monster appears, he ditches the jacket and flees. While trying to escape he trips, and falls to pieces after hitting the ground.

Gearhead briefly appears in "Rickternal Friendshine of the Spotless Mort" in a flashback inside Birdperson's mind, at the funeral of Gearhead's brother Geardude. Rick also reveals that the core reason why he and Birdperson hang out with Gearhead is because he's their fallen comrade's brother.

Gearhead makes another appearance in "How Poopy Got His Poop Back", when Rick gathers some of his friends for an intervention for Mr. Poopybutthole, with Gearhead being the first one to be recruited. After gathering the group fully, they head to a bar called F#%K YOU'S, where they begin drinking and partying. Afterwards they hit several other locations, eventually meeting up with Hugh Jackman (who turns out to be Gene's brother-in-law) and moving their party to his house. After deciding that the crew is going to find Mr. Poopybutthole's ex-wife Amy, Gearhead and the others proceed to her house, where they discover she's having an affair with Predator P.I., who begins hunting them. During a confrontation with the Predator, Gearhead tries to talk his way out of the situation, but gets his head torn off and his "gearsticles" shoved in his mouth again, and wonders aloud why this keeps happening to him. After Mr. Poopybutthole's situation is resolved, Gearhead takes a "fading pill" and goes back to the Gear System, where he gets a spinal replacement.
